sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, you want sweetness to be kept in check.
sections.subtitleText: 0.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, you want sweetness to be kept in check.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 15.2g of carbs. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for fruit, the real question is whether the sweetness stays under control.
sections.subtitleText: 15.2g of carbs. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for fruit, the real question is whether the sweetness stays under control.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 0.4g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. for a fruit, sugar moderation is the whole game.

sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: usually beats ultra-processed sweet snacks because that replacement value matters in real life. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. ingredient simplicity is hard to argue with because whole-fruit clarity is part of the category appeal.

sections.subtitleText: the upsides first: usually beats ultra-processed sweet snacks because that replacement value matters in real life. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. ingredient simplicity is hard to argue with because whole-fruit clarity is part of the category appeal.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: easy to eat quickly when it is very sweet because that can blur the line between light and sugar-heavy portions. satiety is not especially strong because fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal. protein contribution is tiny because that is normal for fruit, but it still limits nutritional breadth.

closing.summary: Pear does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
